brazil.note: Brazil is Latin America's largest e-commerce market, and many retailers and streaming services geo-restrict catalogs and pricing to Brazilian IPs, so local residential IPs are needed for accurate data.

argentina.note: Argentina's currency controls and frequent price volatility mean local IPs are important for capturing accurate peso pricing that differs sharply from prices shown to foreign visitors.

colombia.note: Colombia is one of the fastest-growing e-commerce markets in the Andean region, and retailers like Exito and Falabella localize promotions by region, so Colombian IPs are used for market-price monitoring.

chile.note: Chile has the highest internet penetration in South America and mature online retail, so Chilean IPs are commonly used to track competitive pricing across Falabella, Ripley and Paris.

peru.note: Peru's online retail is concentrated in Lima and dominated by Falabella and Ripley, and local IPs help capture region-specific shipping and pricing that vary from foreign views.

venezuela.note: Venezuela has heavy state internet controls and periodic blocking of sites, so local IPs are used to reach content and pricing that is otherwise filtered or geo-restricted for outside visitors.

ecuador.note: Ecuador uses the US dollar, and while pricing is stable, local IPs are still needed to access region-locked promotions and classifieds listings restricted to Ecuadorian visitors.

bolivia.note: Bolivia has lower e-commerce penetration and much retail happens via classifieds and social marketplaces, so local IPs are used mainly for accessing region-restricted listings and ad content.

paraguay.note: Paraguay is a smaller bilingual market where much commerce runs through classifieds like Clasipar, and local IPs are needed to view listings and offers geo-limited to Paraguayan users.

uruguay.note: Uruguay's fixed broadband is dominated by the state operator Antel, and Uruguayan IPs are used to access local classifieds like Gallito and region-restricted retail pricing.

guyana.note: Guyana is a small English-speaking market with a fast-growing oil economy, and local IPs are used to reach region-restricted classifieds and news content limited to Guyanese visitors.

suriname.note: Suriname is a Dutch-speaking market with fixed and mobile service dominated by state operator Telesur, and local IPs help access region-limited local media and classifieds.

nigeria.note: Nigeria is Africa's largest e-commerce and mobile market, and platforms like Jumia and Jiji localize by region, so Nigerian IPs are essential for accurate price and listing data.

south-africa.note: South Africa is the continent's most developed online retail market led by Takealot, and many services geo-restrict streaming and pricing, so local IPs are needed for market data.

egypt.note: Egypt is the largest e-commerce market in North Africa, and retailers geo-price in Egyptian pounds while some content is filtered, so local IPs are used for accurate pricing and access.

kenya.note: Kenya's e-commerce is tightly linked to Safaricom's M-Pesa mobile money, and many checkout and pricing flows only work correctly from Kenyan IPs.

ghana.note: Ghana has a rapidly growing mobile-first internet economy dominated by MTN, and local IPs are used to access region-restricted classifieds and localized retail pricing.

morocco.note: Morocco's online market runs heavily through classifieds like Avito, and content is often bilingual, so local IPs are used to view listings and offers geo-limited to Moroccan users.

algeria.note: Algeria's online commerce centers on the classifieds site Ouedkniss, and periodic internet restrictions make Algerian IPs useful for reaching local listings and content reliably.

tunisia.note: Tunisia's e-commerce leans on classifieds like Tayara, and local bilingual content and pricing are best captured with Tunisian IPs.

ethiopia.note: Ethiopia's telecom sector was long a state monopoly under Ethio Telecom and has seen periodic internet shutdowns, so local IPs are used to reach region-restricted listings and content.

tanzania.note: Tanzania is a fast-growing mobile-money market where much retail runs through classifieds, and local IPs help access region-limited listings and Swahili-language content.

uganda.note: Uganda has occasionally imposed social media and internet taxes and shutdowns, so Ugandan IPs are used to reliably reach local classifieds and geo-restricted content.

angola.note: Angola is a Portuguese-speaking market dominated by mobile operator Unitel, and local IPs are used to access region-restricted classifieds and localized pricing in kwanza.

cameroon.note: Cameroon is a bilingual market where much commerce runs through classifieds like CoinAfrique, and local IPs are used to view listings and content geo-limited to Cameroonian users.
